title: blechnum melanocaulon subsp. melanocaulon
intro: Blechnum melanocaulon subsp. melanocaulon, also known as the black tree fern, is a species of fern native to New Zealand. It is used in traditional medicine to treat various ailments, including respiratory issues and skin conditions, due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits of Blechnum melanocaulon subsp. melanocaulon include reducing inflammation, fighting infection, and promoting wound healing. Herbal preparations of the plant are typically made by infusing the fronds in hot water to create a tea or decoction, which can be used to treat a range of health issues.
